findings.evidence: `RootsOfUnity.lean:84` states `(Multiplicative (ZMod n) →* Aˣ) ≃* rootsOfUnity n A`; Mathlib’s inputs only need monoid structure: `RingTheory/RootsOfUnity/Basic.lean:310-312` has `[CommGroup G']`, and `Basic.lean:129-130` has `rootsOfUnityUnitsMulEquiv (M) [CommMonoid M]`.
findings.fix: Move `characterMulEquivRootsOfUnity` and its apply/symm lemmas to a `{A : Type*} [CommMonoid A]` section, then use that specialization from the later semiring/algebra points APIs.
findings.issue: `characterMulEquivRootsOfUnity` is now public but inherits `[CommSemiring A]` from the algebraic-group points section even though the statement only concerns monoid characters and `rootsOfUnity n A`.

summary: The base-change specialization itself follows the existing roots-of-unity and split-torus pattern, but the newly exposed character-to-roots equivalence is stated at an unnecessarily strong semiring level.
